title: zygophyllum lahovarii
intro: Zygophyllum lahovarii, also known as the 'Gum Resin Plant', is a succulent shrub native to the Middle East and North Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its gum resin, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the gum resin has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, digestive problems, and respiratory infections, offering benefits such as improved wound healing, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function. In herbal preparations, the gum resin is often used to make ointments, salves, and infusions, which can be applied topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
